post.summary: Get campaign performance metrics over time (graph)
post.description:
Returns time-bucketed data points representing campaign performance over the requested date range.

**Output-point count by range**
- 1–11 days (hourly): always **24 points** — hourly slots are merged so that each output point covers an equal number of hours.
- 12–24 days (daily): **one point per day** — no merging, output count equals the number of calendar days.
- 25–360 days (daily): **≤ 24 points** — consecutive days are merged into buckets using the smallest factor that keeps the output count ≤ 24.
    - Example: if the range is 44 days, the system will merge days into 2-day buckets to produce 22 output points.
    - If the range is 75 days, it will use 4-day buckets to produce 19 output points.
- > 360 days (daily): same as above, with the first N buckets absorbing one extra day each to distribute the remainder evenly.
- Missing time slots (no DB data for a slot) are gap-filled with zero-value data points so the response always covers the full requested range.

requestBody.description: Filters, dimension, and optional timezone for the metrics-timeline graph.

30-day daily graph with filters.summary: Daily breakdown for a month with campaign and owner filters

Empty response for app/workspace owner w.summary: No-data response when user has no effective ownership scope
Empty response for app/workspace owner w.description: Empty response for app/workspace owner with no accessible OW IDs

post.summary: Compare aggregated campaign metrics between two periods
post.description:
Returns aggregated performance metrics for the selected date range (current period)
and for the immediately preceding period of the exact same duration (previous period).

Comparison logic:
- If the selected range is 1 day, response compares "today vs yesterday".
- If the selected range is 7 days, response compares "this 7‑day period vs previous 7‑day period".
- In general, previous period = [startTimeEpochMillis - duration, startTimeEpochMillis),
  where duration = endTimeEpochMillis - startTimeEpochMillis.

The API supports rich filtering on campaigns, creatives, IOs, verticals, statuses and owners.
Data is fetched from reporting.tblr_campaigns and is filtered partition‑first on ow_id and ts_epoch_hourly.

**Ownership rules and owIds behaviour**
- If filters.owIds is provided, it is validated against the logged‑in user's ownership configuration.
- If filters.owIds is omitted or empty, the system will automatically expand it to:
  (a) all customer OW IDs the user is allowed to see, plus
  (b) the logged‑in user's own OW ID.
- For app / workspace owners, if after validation there are no effective OW IDs,
  the API returns a successful response with all metrics set to 0 for both periods.

**Validation rules**
- filters: mandatory object.
- filters.startTimeEpochMillis: required, must be < endTimeEpochMillis.
- filters.endTimeEpochMillis: required, must be > startTimeEpochMillis.
- dimension: required, must be one of the supported values of DashboardDimension
  (e.g. CAMPAIGN, IO, CUSTOMER, WORKSPACE, EXCHANGE). Any other value results
  in HTTP 422 with an appropriate error message.

**Search behaviour (searchField)**
- When dimension = CAMPAIGN, the free‑text search is applied on campaign fields

**Response semantics**
- data.current: metrics aggregated over [startTimeEpochMillis, endTimeEpochMillis).
- data.previous: metrics aggregated over the immediately preceding window of the same duration.

requestBody.description: Filters and aggregation dimension to compute current vs previous period metrics.

Single day, campaign dimension, minimal.summary: Today vs yesterday for a single campaign
Single day, campaign dimension, minimal.description: Compares metrics for one campaign for a 1‑day window against the previous day.

Seven‑day range with rich filters.summary: Week‑over‑week comparison for multiple campaigns and owners
Seven‑day range with rich filters.description: Compares a 7‑day period vs the previous 7‑day period with campaign, status and owner filters.

Invalid range (start \u003E= end).description: Demonstrates a request that will fail validation because the start time is not less than end time.

Invalid dimension value.description: Request using an unsupported dimension value; results in HTTP 422 with an Invalid dimension message.

Empty metrics for app/workspace owner wi.summary: All‑zero metrics when user has no effective ownership scope
Empty metrics for app/workspace owner wi.description: Empty metrics for app/workspace owner with no accessible OW IDs

post.summary: Get campaign performance report grouped by workspace (v4).
post.description: Fetches aggregated campaign performance metrics grouped by workspace owner (owId). Only Super IQM users are authorised. When owIds is empty the API auto-populates all customer owIds of the logged-in super user. Supports AGGREGATED, AGGREGATED_LIST, and AGGREGATED_TOTAL request types.

post.summary: Download workspace report based on filters
post.description:
Downloads a workspace-level campaign performance report in the specified file format based on the provided filters.

**When to use this API:**
- Use this endpoint to export workspace-level campaign performance data for offline analysis or reporting.
- The API returns a pre-signed URL that is valid for a limited time to download the generated file.

**Supported file formats:**
- `xlsx` - Microsoft Excel format
- `csv` - Comma-separated values format

**Supported column fields and labels:**
| Label | Field Value |
|-------|-------------|
| ID | workspaceId |
| Workspace Name | workspaceName |
| Insertion Order Name | ioName |
| Daily Budget | budgetDay |
| Total Budget | budgetTotal |
| Bid Shading Saving | organizationBidShadingSaving |
| Workspace Media Earnings | workspaceMediaEarning |
| Total Workspace Earnings | workspaceTotalEarning |
| Workspace PLD Insights Earnings | workspacePLDInsightsEarnings |
| Workspace SLS Insights Earnings | workspaceSLSInsightsEarnings |
| Workspace VLD Insights Earnings | workspaceVLDInsightsEarnings |
| Workspace Bid Shading Earnings | workspaceBidShadingEarning |
| Workspace AQ Insights Earnings | workspaceAQInsightsEarnings |
| Platform Media Earnings | platformMediaEarning |
| Total Platform Earnings | platformTotalEarning |
| Platform PLD Insights Earnings | platformPLDInsightsEarnings |
| Platform SLS Insights Earnings | platformSLSInsightsEarnings |
| Platform VLD Insights Earnings | platformVLDInsightsEarnings |
| Platform Bid Shading Earnings | platformBidShadingEarning |
| Platform AQ Insights Earnings | platformAQInsightsEarnings |
| Impressions | impressions |
| Clicks | clicks |
| CTR | CTR |
| Win Rate | winRate |
| VCR | VCR |
| Total Advertiser Spent | spent |
| Ad Serving Cost | adServingCost |
| Data Cost | dataCost |
| Pre-bid Cost | prebidCost |
| Media Cost | mediaSpent |
| 3rd Party Cost | thirdPartyCost |
| PLD Insights Cost | pldInsightsCost |
| SLS Insights Cost | slsInsightsCost |
| VLD Insights Cost | vldInsightsCost |
| AQ Insights Cost | aqInsightsCost |
| Workspace Spent | workspaceSpent |
| Platform Spent | platformSpent |
| Attributed Conversions | totalAttributedConversion |
| Attributed View Through Conversions | totalAttributedViewThroughConversion |
| Attributed Click Through Conversions | totalAttributedClickThroughConversion |
| Cost Per Attributed Conversion | costPerAttributedConversion |
| Attributed Conversion Rate | totalAttributedConversionRate |

**Pagination:**
- This endpoint does **not** support pagination parameters. All matching records based on the applied filters are included in the downloaded file.
- Sorting is supported via the `sort` object with `field` (column name) and `order` (`ASC` or `DESC`).

**Response URL:**
- On success, the response contains a pre-signed download URL in `data.url`.
- The URL is a temporary, pre-signed cloud storage link (e.g., AWS S3) that expires after a limited period.
- The client should initiate the file download promptly after receiving the URL.

post.summary: Get campaign performance report grouped by customer/advertiser (v4).
post.description: Fetches aggregated campaign performance metrics grouped by advertiser owId. Accessible by workspace owners and Super IQM users. Last-level advertisers (who have no sub-customers) receive a 403 Forbidden response. When owIds is empty the API auto-populates all child (advertiser-level) owIds of the logged-in user. Workspace-level owIds supplied in the request are silently dropped — only advertiser (leaf) owIds are used for aggregation. Supports AGGREGATED, AGGREGATED_LIST, and AGGREGATED_TOTAL request types.

This API fetches campaign's goal-ai Goal's performance by campaign

get.summary: Get the Goal-AI Optimization Activities for Campaign
get.description:
Retrieves detailed log of Goal-AI optimization activities and decisions for a campaign.
            
            **Use Case**: Understand what optimization actions Goal-AI has taken and why, providing transparency into AI decision-making.
            
            **What it does**:
            - Lists all optimization activities performed by Goal-AI
            - Shows bid adjustments, budget reallocations, targeting changes, and creative optimizations
            - Provides timestamps and reasons for each optimization action
            - Displays predicted vs. actual impact of optimizations
            - Enables audit trail of AI decision-making
            
            **Activity Types**:
            - Bid adjustments (raised/lowered bids on specific dimensions)
            - Budget reallocation across campaigns or line items
            - Targeting modifications (geo, device, audience changes)
            - Creative performance optimizations
            - Pacing adjustments
            - Goal threshold updates
            
            **Returns**: Chronological log of optimization activities with action details and performance impact
            
            **Business Value**: Provides transparency and explainability for AI-driven optimizations, building trust and enabling manual oversight.

parameters.name: dayEpoch
parameters.description: Day Epoch for which activities are to be fetched

get.summary: Get the Goal-AI Optimization Activities for Campaign Download in a File
get.description:
Downloads Goal-AI optimization activities as a file in the specified format (CSV, Excel, PDF).
            
            **Use Case**: Export Goal-AI optimization history for offline analysis, reporting, or sharing with stakeholders.
            
            **What it does**:
            - Generates downloadable file containing all Goal-AI optimization activities
            - Supports multiple file formats (CSV, XLSX, PDF)
            - Includes same data as getGoalAIOptimizationActivities endpoint
            - Formats data for readability and analysis in external tools
            - Streams file to client for download
            
            **File Formats**:
            - CSV: For data analysis in Excel, Python, R
            - XLSX: For formatted Excel reports with multiple sheets
            - PDF: For presentation-ready optimization reports
            
            **Returns**: File stream with optimization activities in requested format
            
            **Business Value**: Enables offline analysis, stakeholder reporting, and integration with external BI tools for Goal-AI performance review.

parameters.name: dayEpoch
parameters.description: Day Epoch for which activities are to be fetched

parameters.name: fileType
parameters.description: File type for download - currently supported file type is 'csv'

ExchangeReportRequest.description: Request for generating an exchange-level report, including pagination, sorting, filters, and report type

CustomerReportDownloadRequest.description: Request model for downloading customer reports, extending the base CustomerReportRequest with additional download parameters

CampaignReportRequest.description: Request for generating a campaign performance report, aggregated by campaign.

CampaignReportRecords.description: Campaign Records fields for Success data

totalECPC.description: Effective total cost per click (including all cost components)

totalECPM.description: Effective total cost per thousand impressions

audioVideoViewed.description: Total audio/video watch time in seconds

budgetPacing.description: Whether budget pacing is enabled for the campaign

bidShading.description: Whether bid shading is enabled for the campaign

ioStartTime.description: Insertion order start time, epoch milliseconds

ioEndTime.description: Insertion order end time, epoch milliseconds

isAutoSumIoTotalBudget.description: Whether the IO total budget is auto-summed from its campaigns

modelledDimensionIds.description: Modelled dimension IDs used for rule-based optimization

items.description: Modelled dimension IDs used for rule-based optimization

creativesCount.description: Number of creatives associated with the campaign

isEligibleForAQInsightsReport.description: Whether the campaign is eligible for the AQ insights report

isEligibleForPLDInsightsReport.description: Whether the campaign is eligible for the PLD insights report

isEligibleForSLSInsightsReport.description: Whether the campaign is eligible for the SLS insights report

isEligibleForVLDInsightsReport.description: Whether the campaign is eligible for the VLD insights report

isEligibleForICTInsightsReport.description: Whether the campaign is eligible for the ICT insights report

isEligibleForNLDInsightsReport.description: Whether the campaign is eligible for the NLD insights report

campaignPriority.description: Campaign priority (only populated when campaignRequest = true)

bidModelDataId.description: Bid model data ID (only populated when campaignRequest = true)

additionalProperties.description: Campaign Pulse diagnosis result mapping severity to check counts

campaignPulseResult.description: Campaign Pulse diagnosis result mapping severity to check counts

eCPM.description: Effective media cost per thousand impressions

eCPCV.description: Effective media cost per completed video view

CampaignReportResponse.description: Detailed campaign report row, extending the base ReportResponse with campaign-specific metadata, IO details, budget/pacing helper fields, insights costs/earnings and eligibility flags.

campaignRequest.description: Whether this request is scoped to a specific campaign

CampaignReportDownloadRequest.description: Request for downloading a campaign performance report as a file.

DashboardCreateRequest.description: Request body to create a new dashboard configuration

id.description: Identifier of the newly created dashboard configuration

DashboardCreateData.description: Data payload for a successful dashboard creation

ioId.description: Optional insertion order id to filter campaigns

campaignIds.description: Optional list of campaign ids to restrict response

items.description: Optional list of campaign ids to restrict response

CampaignIOBasicDetailsRequestVO.description: Request payload for fetching campaign basic details for a workspace, with optional IO and campaign-id filters.

CampaignPulseCategoryListResponse.description: Campaign Pulse Category List Response Wrapper

category.description: Display name of the campaign pulse category

CampaignPulseCategoryVO.description: Represents a campaign pulse category, containing its display name and display order.

DashboardConfigurations.description: Docs-only schema for the dashboard configuration list response

isSelectingCampaignsByGroup.description: Whether campaigns are being selected by group

Data.description: Config and metadata for the table and graphs

value.description: Field key used for the graph

description.example: Click-through rate is the percentage of Clicks received per the Impressions served

dependentDimensions.description: Dependent dimensions (nullable). May be null or an array.

CampaignPulseSeverityListResponse.description: Campaign Pulse Severity List Response Wrapper

additionalProperties.description: Map of severity level to diagnosis check count

severityCount.description: Map of severity level to diagnosis check count

CampaignPulseSeverityResponse.description: Represents the severity response for a campaign pulse, containing the campaign ID and a map of severity counts.

diagnosisResults.description: Ordered list of diagnosis checks returned for the campaign

lastRun.description: Epoch milliseconds of the last campaign pulse run

CampaignPulseResponse.description: Response model for the campaign pulse API, containing campaign-level pulse metadata and an ordered list of diagnosis checks.

checkId.description: Unique identifier of the diagnosis check

checkLabel.description: Human-readable label for the diagnosis check

checkDescription.description: Description of what the diagnosis check evaluates
checkDescription.example: CTR is below the expected threshold for this campaign type.

recommendedActions.description: Recommended actions to address the diagnosis result
recommendedActions.example: Refresh creatives and validate audience targeting.

category.description: Category the diagnosis check belongs to

agentName.description: Name of the agent that produced the diagnosis check

order.description: Display order of the diagnosis check

DiagnosisResult.description: Represents a single diagnosis check in the campaign pulse response.
